core_engine: dread accumulates through wrongness the body registers before the mind names it

chapter_1_beats.beat_text: Sora Akiyama stands very still in the dark a narrow rented apartment where the hallway light flickers on its own schedule and states, with calm certainty, that something is here with them tonight. The caption is steady. The art holds one detail — a door open a hand's width — that they do not remark on.
They move through the rooms switching on every light, narrating the layout the way you'd brief someone on a threat. Each lamp pushes the shadow back one step; the shadow returns the moment they pass.
A sound that has an ordinary explanation. Sora Akiyama supplies the frightening one instead — confidently, and a little too fast. The reader notes the speed before they do.
The presence, glimpsed — never whole. A wrongness at the edge of the mirror, a pressure on the back of the neck. The panel itself warps faintly, as if the room is breathing the way they are.

the night-shift archivist who has seen this dread before and is unafraid of it sits in the lit kitchen and does not argue that the house is empty. 'I'm not going to tell you nothing's there. I'm going to ask you to breathe while it is.' Their hands stay folded, unhurried.
Sora Akiyama stops bracing for one held panel. No lights, no checking the corners. They simply stay in the room with the presence and do not run. Nothing attacks. The waiting itself thins.

In the not-running, the shape resolves a fraction. The thing in the house has the line of their own shoulders, their own held breath. Recognition begins — unstated, because stating it would end it. Held in silence.

The cost surfaces. the night-shift archivist who has seen this dread before and is unafraid of it admits, in one short line, how long they have watched Sora Akiyama live braced like this. It reads as either comfort or warning. Sora Akiyama has no fast answer for the first time.

Ambiguous close. Close-up on Sora Akiyama's face, calmer — and the hallway behind them, the door now shut, though no one is shown closing it. One thing the reader holds that the protagonist's hypothesis cannot. No resolution.
